explanation: The '-sd' parameter is a valid substitution for '-SecurityDescriptorSddl' in this context. This command is functionally equivalent and may bypass detection by appearing different in the command line.
This is a direct copy of the true positive command. It is included for completeness and to show the baseline behavior.
This command uses string concatenation to obfuscate the SDDL string. It is functionally identical but may evade detection by not matching the 'DCLCWPDTSD' substring directly.
This command uses PowerShell string manipulation to generate the SDDL string. It is a more complex obfuscation technique that may evade simple substring-based detection.
This command uses an environment variable to store the SDDL string. This technique may bypass detection by not including the string directly in the command line.
This command uses Base64 encoding to obfuscate the SDDL string. It may evade detection by not containing the string in plain text.
